title: Automatic creation of storage.conf/libpod.conf is an anti-feature
body:
This effectively introduces the requirement that all users running podman either have a writable home directory or are provisioned in advance. This is not always the case, especially at scale with lots of "worker" users.

The cost is clear but I see no benefit to this approach. Is there something I'm missing?

comments.body: I presume this specific use case was why the `--storage-opt` setting was added. (Though I sorely miss an equivalent `--libpod-opt`.)
I believe we've already disabled this - @rhatdan can you confirm?
Ok, this is my mistake. I didn't rehash my shell after `sudo make install`. You did remove this feature. Sorry!

There still seem to be some directories that podman is trying to create, but the upside is that at least it can still run under 2.0.5.

I'm running the following command:
`podman --root /podman/storage --runroot /podman/run --tmpdir /podman/tmp run -it alpine sh`

As expected, I get dropped into `ash`, but I still get:
`ERRO[0000] unable to make temp dir /home/<dummy>/.config`

I believe [this line](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/pkg/util/utils_supported.go#L86) is the culprit.

It should have also been removed as part of the changes discussed above no? (For the same reasons at least.)

Yes that should have been removed before.

@jjzmajic Nice catch.
